VILLA SERENA HEALTHCARE CENTER

Address
723 E 9TH STREET, LONG BEACH, CA 90813

Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.52 3.48 5.02 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.27 0.61 0.30 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.46 0.77 1.63 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.79 2.10 3.09 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.73 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.85
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.18
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.28
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
36.8%
annual
RN turnover
62.5%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.23
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.89
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-18 8 6 2 44 0 44
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-21 11 8 3 84 1 84
Total weighted health score
54.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (43)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
37 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-25 2025-12-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-28 2025-04-16
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. J 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0773 Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0800 Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. F 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. F 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2025-01-18 2025-02-12
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-31 2024-06-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-05-06 2024-05-24
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. F 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. B 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-01-21 2024-02-20
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2021-11-17 2021-12-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2021-11-17 2021-12-14
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2021-11-17 2021-12-14
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2021-11-17 2021-12-14
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. B 2021-11-17 2021-12-14
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2021-11-17 2021-12-14

Deficiencies 0698 — tag 0698 0698.tag
Deficiencies 0755 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0755.correction_date
Deficiencies 0755 — description Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. 0755.description
Deficiencies 0755 — scope_severity D 0755.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0755 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0755.survey_date
Deficiencies 0755 — tag 0755 0755.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity E 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0759 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0759.correction_date
Deficiencies 0759 — description Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. 0759.description
Deficiencies 0759 — scope_severity E 0759.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0759 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0759.survey_date
Deficiencies 0759 — tag 0759 0759.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity E 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag

Deficiencies 0770 — description Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. 0770.description
Deficiencies 0770 — scope_severity D 0770.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0770 — tag 0770 0770.tag
Deficiencies 0773 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0773.correction_date
Deficiencies 0773 — description Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. 0773.description
Deficiencies 0773 — scope_severity D 0773.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0773 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0773.survey_date
Deficiencies 0773 — tag 0773 0773.tag
Deficiencies 0800 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0800.correction_date
Deficiencies 0800 — description Provide each resident with a nourishing, palatable, well-balanced diet that meets his or her daily nutritional and special dietary needs. 0800.description
Deficiencies 0800 — scope_severity D 0800.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0800 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0800.survey_date
Deficiencies 0800 — tag 0800 0800.tag
Deficiencies 0803 — correction_date 2025-02-12 0803.correction_date
Deficiencies 0803 — description Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. 0803.description
Deficiencies 0803 — scope_severity E 0803.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0803 — survey_date 2025-01-18 0803.survey_date

Deficiencies 0805 — description Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. 0805.description
